definition: The blocker is a rare Indic syllable-reordering condition tested once per syllable; context-sensitive coverage logs each foreach_syllable invocation as a distinct (call-context, edge) slot, manufacturing new-coverage signals per syllable that accumulate pressure on the rare glyph-category configuration until it is satisfied.

authoring_contract.goal: ONE parameterized template.c isolating this cluster's shared mechanism, with exactly ONE compile-time -D knob = the program-feature axis. params.json:parameter MUST name that macro; scan_values sweep it; fuzzers = involved_fuzzers; expected_direction = '<winner> > <loser>'.
authoring_contract.isolate: No partial-match coverage edges unless the mechanism IS a gradient (VP families). Keep the gate the only objective.

members.function: hb-ot-shaper-indic.cc:final_reordering_syllable_indic(hb_ot_shape_plan_t const*, hb_buffer_t*, unsigned int, unsigned int)

signature.mechanism_summary: CtxHook records each syllable-segmentation invocation of final_reordering at a distinct (call-context, edge) slot, rewarding inputs that reach the deep reordering path through new contexts and thereby steering toward the rare base>POS_BASE_C configuration.
signature.one_line: per-invocation context buckets drive corpus toward rare Indic base position condition

analysis.summary_one_line: A multi-glyph Indic syllable whose base consonant ends with indic_position above POS_BASE_C, reached via a deep reordering call context.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: An input decoded into an Indic syllable with at least 2 glyphs where, after base-finding, the base glyph's indic_position() exceeds POS_BASE_C and start < base — i.e. multi-consonant clusters that drive the final_reordering machinery past the simple-base case into the matra/reph reordering paths.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The winner-resolving seeds are dense repeated-byte glyph runs (e.g. 22-byte seed of repeated 0x75 'u' plus 0x97 runs) that decode to long uniform syllables, exercising the deep reordering paths; naive_ctx folds the call-context into the edge map, so reaching L1099 via the specific syllable-segmentation/base-finding call path registers as NEW coverage and is retained, repeatedly steering mutation toward the rare base>POS_BASE_C configuration (winner resolved=10/10).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: The loser (naive) sees only the bare edge at L1099, which it already covers on the False side (T=0 F=17 in one variant), so the rare True side earns no novelty credit; its blocking seeds are sparse 0x09/0x0c/0x00 patterns that stay in the simple-base / no-pre-base-matra regime and it blocks 8/10 trials.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx's CtxHook folds a running hash of the caller chain into the edge-map index, so the same L1099 edge counts as distinct coverage per call context; this context-sensitive coverage rewards seeds that reach the deep reordering path through new call contexts, driving naive_ctx to retain and extend them while plain naive gives the bare edge no novelty.

evidence_trail.claim: The decisive pair is naive_ctx > naive with delta ctx_coverage, prob_div 0.80.
The winner resolved all 10 trials while the loser blocked 8 of 10.
The blocking branch requires start < base and base's indic_position above POS_BASE_C.
The blocked True side of L1099 is rarely taken; loser shows T=0 on one variant while winner reaches T=2.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L1099  T=2 F=0  T=0 F=17  if (start < base && info[base].indic_position() > POS_BAS...  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: Winner-resolving seeds are dense repeated-glyph byte runs that decode to long uniform syllables.
Loser-blocking seeds are sparse 0x09/0x0c/0x00 patterns lacking the dense glyph runs.
naive_ctx folds the call context into the edge-map index so the same edge is recorded at different slots depending on call path.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
folds it into the edge-map index, so the same basic-block edge is
recorded at different map slots depending on the call path that
reached it.

evidence_trail.claim: For naive_ctx a new coverage bucket is a new (call-context, edge) pair rather than a bare edge, unlike naive.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
a "new bucket" is a new
(call-context, edge) pair rather than a bare edge.

evidence_trail.claim: Plain naive uses a per-edge hit-count bucket with no context indexing, so a covered edge yields no further novelty.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
per-edge hit-count bucket; a new bucket triggers a
corpus-add (LibAFL `MaxMapFeedback` over the edge map).

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating only on info[base].indic_position() > POS_BASE_C in which naive_ctx and naive resolve the True side at statistically indistinguishable rates — showing the context-indexed edge map provided no advantage at this branch.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The seed-byte interpretation (dense repeated-glyph runs decode into the multi-consonant syllable that produces base>POS_BASE_C) is inferred from byte patterns rather than from a decoded glyph trace, so the exact glyph-level cause is not directly observed.

members.function: hb-ot-shaper-indic.cc:final_reordering_syllable_indic(hb_ot_shape_plan_t const*, hb_buffer_t*, unsigned int, unsigned int)

signature.mechanism_summary: CtxHook logs the final-reordering edge at different map slots per foreach_syllable call context, manufacturing new-coverage signals that accumulate inputs probing the deep reorder arm until one satisfies the ZWJ category test at new_pos+1.
signature.one_line: per-syllable context slots accumulate corpus until halant+ZWJ position condition satisfied

analysis.summary_one_line: A multi-syllable Indic buffer whose pre-base matra search lands new_pos+1 on a ZWJ glyph following a halant, deep inside per-syllable reordering.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Bytes decoding to an Indic syllable where the pre-base matra back-scan (L1152-1154) lands on a halant whose immediately following glyph at new_pos+1 has indic_category ZWJ, so info[new_pos + 1].indic_category() == I_Cat(ZWJ) is true. This requires a halant+ZWJ sequence positioned correctly within a non-Malayalam/Tamil syllable, reached only after the deep reordering prologue (base-finding, matra-reorder guard) succeeds.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx resolves the branch in 10/10 trials with avg hitcount 14 on the branch; its per-role coverage drives the true-side block at L1171-1177 ([W] new_pos--; goto search). The CtxHook folds the call context into the edge map, so each foreach_syllable invocation of final_reordering_syllable_indic is recorded as a distinct (call-context, edge) bucket, rewarding inputs that exercise the reordering path under varied syllable contexts and so reaching the ZWJ-after-halant arm.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive resolves only 2/10 (blocked 8/10) with avg hitcount 1; at L1170 it records W(T/F) 21/1 vs L(T/F) 0/21 — naive never takes the true side. Naive's bare per-edge MaxMapFeedback treats the blocker edge as already-covered once any syllable reaches L1170 false, so it gets no new-coverage reward for steering toward the rare halant+ZWJ configuration and stalls (avg duration blocked 21.40h vs winner 4.80h).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx's CtxHook context-sensitive coverage: the same L1170 edge is logged at different map slots per call context (per syllable / call path), so distinct syllable arrangements count as new coverage and the corpus accumulates inputs that probe the deep reordering arm until one satisfies the ZWJ category test. Naive lacks this granularity (identical havoc/token mutators, no I2S, no CMP_MAP), so the divergence is attributable to the single technique delta: context-sensitive coverage.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the true side of a ZWJ category test deep inside per-syllable Indic reordering.
The branch is reached once per syllable via a foreach_syllable loop in the 1-hop caller.
The winning true-side block (keep searching) is hit only by the winner-resolving role.
naive_ctx resolves the branch in all 10 trials while naive blocks in 8.
The loser (naive) never takes the true side at L1170; the winner does 21 times.
naive_ctx differs from naive only by the CtxHook that folds call context into the edge-map index, making (call-context, edge) the coverage unit.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
a "new bucket" is a new
(call-context, edge) pair rather than a bare edge

evidence_trail.claim: naive_ctx uses the same havoc/token mutators as naive with no I2S or CMP_MAP, so the only delta is the coverage granularity.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
naive's havoc + token stack. No `I2SRandReplace`, no
CMP_MAP.

evidence_trail.claim: The decisive pair's technique delta is ctx_coverage and the involved fuzzers are naive and naive_ctx.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness with the same halant+ZWJ reordering branch reached via a single (non-looped, context-invariant) call site shows no naive_ctx-over-naive advantage — i.e. removing the per-syllable call-context multiplicity eliminates the divergence, proving the win came from something other than context-sensitive coverage granularity.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: No seed-byte chain proves a specific input field decodes to the halant+ZWJ glyph sequence at new_pos+1; the byte-diff offsets are PARTIAL and the link from input bytes to the ZWJ category is inferred from source structure, not demonstrated.
